prompt: Explain what TensorRT-LLM does in two sentences.
Give three practical reasons to benchmark LLM inference instead of only checking that generation works.
In plain language, define latency and throughput for an LLM serving system.
What is KV cache in transformer inference, and why does it matter?
Summarize the difference between FP16, BF16, and FP8 for inference in a compact paragraph.

Summarize this text in four bullets: TensorRT-LLM compiles and runs transformer models with NVIDIA GPU optimizations. Benchmarking should include a baseline, fixed prompts, warmup, latency percentiles, token throughput, memory use, and clear notes about hardware and software versions. Results should not compare different input and output lengths as if they were the same workload.

Summarize this release-note style paragraph in two sentences: A benchmark run captured the host environment, pulled an NGC TensorRT-LLM container, downloaded a small Qwen model, built a BF16 engine, measured latency and throughput on synthetic request lengths, attempted FP8 KV cache, and generated a final report with quality checks.

Create a concise executive summary from this text: The L40S instance completed the baseline and optimized inference runs. TensorRT-LLM improved generation throughput on longer synthetic prompts. The quality regression suite found no empty outputs and all deterministic expected substrings appeared. Nsight Systems showed GPU kernels dominating steady-state inference.

Summarize the following operational rule in one paragraph: Use the local workstation for editing, parsing, and reporting. Start EC2 only when GPU work is ready. Immediately schedule an in-instance shutdown guard. Stop the instance after the GPU phase finishes or fails. Never leave the GPU instance running for local report generation.

Reduce this checklist to a short paragraph: capture environment, download model, run Hugging Face baseline, build TensorRT-LLM engine, smoke test generation, prepare synthetic datasets, run latency benchmarks, run throughput benchmarks, attempt KV-cache or quantization optimization, run quality regression, parse results, write final report.
Write a Python function percentile(values, pct) that returns the nearest-rank percentile for a non-empty list of numbers. Include a short docstring.
Write a Bash snippet that exits if nvidia-smi is not available, then prints the GPU name and total memory.
Write a Python dataclass named BenchmarkRow with fields variant, dataset, avg_latency_ms, and generation_tokens_per_sec.
Write a Python function normalize_text(s) that lowercases, strips whitespace, and collapses repeated spaces.
Write a shell command that starts an EC2 instance with AWS CLI and waits until it is running. Use placeholder variables for profile, region, and instance id.
A model produces 2400 output tokens in 60 seconds across 30 requests. What is the generation token throughput, and what is the average output length per request? Show the calculation briefly.
If a baseline average latency is 800 ms and an optimized engine average latency is 320 ms on the same dataset, what is the latency speedup?
If baseline GPU memory is 11.2 GB and optimized GPU memory is 7.0 GB, compute the memory reduction ratio used in this project.
Why is it invalid to compare an HF prompt baseline with a TensorRT synthetic benchmark that has different input and output lengths?
A benchmark has 5 warmup requests and 20 measured requests. Why should warmup requests be excluded from the latency summary?

Read this scenario and give a compact action plan: You are preparing a TensorRT-LLM benchmark on a single L40S EC2 instance. The repo is local, the GPU instance is stopped, and the task requires local-first work. The instance must be started only after scripts are ready. If network or API access is lost, a scheduled shutdown guard must stop the instance. The benchmark must include HF baseline, TensorRT-LLM optimized runs, a KV-cache or quantization experiment, quality checks, and final reporting.

Given this benchmark policy, identify the most important engineering controls: Use NVIDIA tools where possible. Use AWS only for raw EC2. Keep EC2 stopped except for GPU phases. Store generated large artifacts locally on the instance data volume. Do not claim speedups without matching workloads. Document model fallbacks. Record exact container tag and digest. Generate reproducible reports.

Analyze this failure case and propose mitigation: During a TensorRT-LLM engine build, the local Codex session disconnects. The EC2 instance is still running. The engineer previously scheduled sudo shutdown -h +240 inside the instance and configured instance-initiated shutdown behavior to stop. AWS API credentials may or may not still be valid when the session returns.

Read the following benchmark result description and list what should appear in the final report: HF baseline completed on Qwen3-1.7B using BF16. TensorRT-LLM base engine completed. FP8 KV-cache build failed because the current CLI did not expose the option for this model, so a documented KV-cache block-size experiment was run. Quality checks passed 19 of 20 deterministic prompts. Nsight Systems showed no NCCL-heavy path on single GPU.

You have four datasets named isl128_osl128, isl512_osl128, isl1024_osl128, and isl2048_osl256. Explain how to present latency and throughput results without mixing workloads. Mention why input length and output length belong in every result row.
Return only the capital of France.
Return only the chemical symbol for water.
Return only the result of 7 + 5.
Return only the largest planet in the Solar System.
Return only the programming language commonly associated with files ending in .py.
Return only the AWS service name for object storage buckets.
Return only the NVIDIA command commonly used to inspect GPU status.
Return only the container registry brand used for NVIDIA GPU containers.
Return only the acronym for time to first token.
Return only the acronym for input sequence length.
Return only the acronym for output sequence length.
Return only the GPU model family used by AWS g6e instances in this project.
Return only the word used for the Python standard virtual environment module.
Return only the name of the version control system used by this repository.
Return only the cloud provider used for EC2.
Return only the model optimization runtime named in TensorRT-LLM.
Return only yes or no: Should warmup requests be included in measured latency percentiles?
Return only yes or no: Should benchmark speedup be claimed without measured matching workloads?
Return only the storage volume type recommended in the task for EBS.
Return only the profiler family requested for this project.
In exactly one short sentence, say why a benchmark should record the container image digest.
In one sentence, state why EC2 should be stopped after GPU work.
In one sentence, state why quality checks are useful after quantization.
In one sentence, state why GPU memory should be reported for LLM inference.
In one sentence, state what TensorRT-LLM is optimizing for in this project.
